message:
perf(audit,events): backward-scan for last entry, logger cache cleanup, query optimization

Audit logger cache management:
- Add background goroutine (10-minute ticker) that closes and removes
  audit loggers unused for 30+ minutes. Long-running NTM sessions
  accumulate one logger per monitored session, and without cleanup
  these hold open file descriptors indefinitely. The lastAccess map
  tracks when each logger was last requested.

Audit last-entry backward scan:
- readLastEntry() now uses ReadAt with a backward-scanning buffer
  instead of bufio.Scanner from the beginning. For large audit logs
  (100K+ lines), the previous approach read the entire file to find
  the last entry. The new approach reads 4KB chunks from the end,
  typically finding the last entry in a single read.
- Added 10MB sanity limit on individual line length to prevent OOM
  on corrupted files.

Audit query optimization:
- searchFile() and BuildIndex() now use scanner.Bytes() instead of
  scanner.Text(), avoiding a string allocation per line. For files
  with millions of lines, this significantly reduces GC pressure.
- extractDateFromFilename() simplified: instead of splitting on dashes
  and reassembling, directly extract the last 10 characters and
  validate the YYYY-MM-DD format.

Audit test fix:
- splitLines() helper in logger_test.go was broken -- it iterated over
  an empty slice literal and had a redundant character-by-character
  reimplementation. Replaced with strings.Split + TrimSpace.

Events logger last-event backward scan:
- LastEvent() now uses the same backward-scanning approach as audit,
  reading from the end of the file instead of scanning all lines.
- Thread safety: copies l.path under lock before opening file.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
